title: OsRng dependency may fail on some embedded targets
description:
File: rust/lichen-oscore/src/edhoc.rs
Lines: 238, 654

Both EdhocInitiator::new and EdhocResponder::new use OsRng for ephemeral key generation:
```rust
let eph_secret = StaticSecret::random_from_rng(rand_core::OsRng);
```

OsRng requires operating system entropy sources, which may not be available on:
- Bare-metal embedded targets (no_std without OS)
- Some RTOS environments without /dev/urandom equivalent
- Early boot stages before entropy is available

The crate is marked #![cfg_attr(not(feature = "std"), no_std)] but the edhoc module uses OsRng unconditionally.

Impact: EDHOC unusable on constrained embedded targets without OS entropy.

Fix: Either:
1. Make EDHOC feature require std
2. Accept an RNG parameter: `fn new<R: CryptoRngCore>(seed: [u8;32], c_i: u8, rng: &mut R)`
3. Accept pre-generated ephemeral keys
